claims.claim: Egg intake up to one egg/day is not associated with increased risk of cardiovascular disease (CVD) when compared to low or no egg intake.
Moderate egg intake is associated with a lower risk of stroke compared to low or no egg intake.
The evidence for the relationship of egg intake with disease risk is based on observational studies, which are susceptible to residual confounding and reverse causation bias.
Increased egg intake (greater than 1 egg/day) may increase serum LDL cholesterol concentrations.
